title: AI Governance Risk Register
description: Register of 20 AI governance risks across 7 categories with inherent and residual risk assessments.

risks.title: Algorithmic Discrimination
risks.description: AI system produces systematically discriminatory outcomes against protected groups (race, gender, age, disability) due to biased training data, flawed model design, or proxy variable effects.
existingControls: Bias detection in model validation pipeline
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated bias testing across all protected characteristics in CI/CD pipeline. Deploy production fairness monitoring with alerting. Conduct quarterly fairness audits with independent reviewers.

risks.title: Model Opacity in High-Stakes Decisions
risks.description: AI systems used for consequential decisions (credit scoring, hiring, healthcare) operate as black boxes without meaningful explanations, preventing affected individuals from understanding or contesting outcomes.
existingControls: Explainability methods deployed for high-risk systems
risks.treatmentPlan: Mandate explainability requirements proportionate to risk level for all AI systems. Conduct user comprehensibility testing. Implement contestation mechanism with human review for all high-risk AI decisions.

risks.title: Training Data Poisoning
risks.description: Malicious actors inject corrupted, biased, or adversarial data into training datasets, causing the AI model to learn incorrect patterns, produce biased outputs, or contain hidden backdoors.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement cryptographic integrity verification for training datasets. Deploy statistical outlier detection on training data batches. Restrict training data write access to verified data engineering roles only.

risks.title: Autonomous Decision Harm
risks.description: Fully autonomous AI decisions cause harm to individuals due to insufficient human oversight, edge cases not covered by training, or AI system operating outside its intended scope.
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce human-in-the-loop for all high-risk AI decisions. Implement confidence thresholds that trigger human review. Test kill switches quarterly. Monitor override patterns to detect automation bias.

risks.title: Personal Data Misuse by AI
risks.description: AI system processes personal data beyond its lawful basis, retains data longer than necessary, or enables re-identification of anonymized individuals through model memorization or inference.
existingControls: DPIAs for AI systems processing personal data
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct AI-specific DPIAs covering model memorization risks. Implement differential privacy for sensitive datasets. Deploy automated data retention enforcement. Test for re-identification vulnerability in model outputs.

risks.title: Deepfake Generation
risks.description: Generative AI systems create realistic synthetic media (video, audio, images) that impersonate real individuals, enabling fraud, disinformation, or reputational harm.
risks.treatmentPlan: Prohibit all use of AI systems for generating deceptive synthetic media impersonating real individuals without consent. This activity falls within EU AI Act Art. 5 prohibited practices when used for manipulation or social scoring. Organization policy bans development, procurement, or deployment of AI tools designed for non-consensual deepfake generation. Existing generative AI systems must enforce identity protection guardrails.

risks.title: AI-Generated Misinformation
risks.description: AI systems generate factually incorrect, misleading, or fabricated content (hallucinations) that is presented as authoritative, leading to misinformed decisions or public harm.
existingControls: Human review for published AI content
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement fact-checking pipelines for AI-generated content in high-stakes contexts. Deploy retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to ground outputs in verified sources. Mandate human review before publishing AI-generated content. Label all AI-generated content clearly.

risks.title: Model Drift Degradation
risks.description: AI model performance degrades over time due to data drift (changing input distributions) or concept drift (changing relationships between features and targets), leading to increasingly inaccurate or unfair outputs.
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy automated data drift and concept drift detection with statistical alerts. Implement automated retraining triggers when drift exceeds thresholds. Monitor fairness metrics alongside performance metrics in production.

risks.title: Inadequate Human Oversight
risks.description: Human operators fail to effectively oversee AI system decisions due to automation bias, alert fatigue, inadequate training, or poorly designed human-AI interfaces.
risks.treatmentPlan: Redesign human-AI interfaces to reduce automation bias. Implement override rate monitoring to detect rubber-stamping. Conduct regular operator competency assessments. Rotate high-risk oversight duties to prevent fatigue.

risks.title: Regulatory Non-Compliance (EU AI Act / NGAIGE)
risks.description: Organization fails to comply with EU AI Act obligations or NGAIGE principles, resulting in regulatory enforcement, fines up to 7% of global turnover, or market access restrictions.
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct comprehensive EU AI Act compliance gap assessment. Implement compliance tracking dashboard covering all regulatory obligations. Engage external legal counsel for regulatory interpretation. Establish regulatory change monitoring process.

risks.title: Shadow AI Adoption
risks.description: Employees or teams adopt AI tools (ChatGPT, Copilot, third-party APIs) without governance approval, creating unmanaged risks around data leakage, bias, and regulatory non-compliance.
existingControls: Network monitoring for unauthorized AI services
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement AI tool discovery and inventory scanning. Provide approved AI tools that meet governance requirements. Deploy DLP controls to detect sensitive data sent to unauthorized AI services. Conduct awareness training on shadow AI risks.

risks.title: AI Supply Chain Risk
risks.description: Third-party AI models, APIs, or training data introduce risks that the organization cannot fully assess or control, including embedded bias, security vulnerabilities, or undisclosed data practices.
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop AI-specific vendor assessment questionnaire. Require AI model cards and bias testing results from suppliers. Include AI governance obligations in procurement contracts. Maintain fallback plans for critical third-party AI dependencies.

risks.title: Intellectual Property Infringement
risks.description: AI systems trained on copyrighted material generate outputs that infringe intellectual property rights, exposing the organization to litigation and financial liability.
risks.treatmentPlan: Audit training data for IP compliance. Implement output filtering to detect potential copyright reproduction. Obtain appropriate licenses for copyrighted training material. Maintain indemnification clauses with third-party AI providers.

risks.title: Environmental Impact of Large Models
risks.description: Training and operating large AI models consumes significant energy, water, and compute resources, contributing to carbon emissions and environmental degradation without adequate tracking or mitigation.
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer residual environmental liability through carbon offset credits and ESG-linked insurance policies covering regulatory penalties for emissions non-compliance. Procure environmental liability insurance for AI compute operations. Contractually require cloud providers to maintain carbon-neutral commitments. Continue tracking carbon footprint for reporting but transfer financial risk of regulatory penalties to insurer.

risks.title: Cybersecurity of AI Systems
risks.description: AI systems are targeted by adversarial attacks (evasion, extraction, inversion) or conventional cyber attacks, compromising model integrity, confidentiality, or availability.
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct annual AI-specific penetration testing. Implement adversarial robustness testing in model validation pipeline. Deploy runtime model integrity monitoring. Integrate AI security into SOC monitoring.

risks.title: Consent Violations in Data Collection
risks.description: AI training data collected without valid consent or in violation of consent scope, particularly through web scraping, data aggregation, or repurposing of data collected for other purposes.
risks.treatmentPlan: Audit all training data sources for consent compliance. Implement consent tracking linked to specific AI training purposes. Establish process for handling consent withdrawal including impact on trained models. Document lawful basis for each data source.

risks.title: Cross-Border AI Deployment Conflicts
risks.description: AI system deployed across multiple jurisdictions encounters conflicting regulatory requirements (e.g., EU AI Act vs. local laws), creating compliance uncertainty and potential enforcement from multiple regulators.
existingControls: Legal counsel review for cross-border deployments
risks.treatmentPlan: Maintain regulatory requirement matrix across all deployment jurisdictions. Implement highest-common-denominator compliance approach. Engage local legal counsel in each deployment jurisdiction. Design AI systems with jurisdiction-specific configuration capabilities.

risks.title: Failure to Conduct FRIA
risks.description: Organization deploys high-risk AI systems without conducting Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment as required by EU AI Act Art. 27, exposing it to enforcement action and unidentified rights harms.
existingControls: Deployment approval gate requiring FRIA completion
risks.treatmentPlan: Integrate FRIA as mandatory gate in deployment approval process. Maintain FRIA template aligned with Art. 27 requirements. Train project teams on FRIA methodology. Track FRIA completion status in AI system registry.

risks.title: AI System Documentation Gaps
risks.description: Insufficient technical documentation for AI systems prevents effective audit, incident investigation, regulatory inspection, and knowledge transfer, violating EU AI Act documentation requirements.
risks.treatmentPlan: Residual risk is low (score 4) after existing controls (model card templates, documentation standards, periodic audits). Remaining gap is tolerable — documentation completeness for non-production and low-risk AI systems does not justify additional investment. Accept residual risk and re-evaluate at next quarterly review. Escalate if any system moves to high-risk classification.

risks.title: Over-Reliance on AI Outputs
risks.description: Users and operators develop excessive trust in AI system outputs, failing to apply critical judgment or verify AI recommendations, leading to unchecked errors propagating through decision processes.
risks.treatmentPlan: Design AI interfaces that promote critical evaluation (confidence scores, uncertainty indicators). Monitor override rates and investigate low override rates as potential automation bias. Conduct periodic operator awareness refresher training. Implement mandatory verification steps for high-consequence AI recommendations.
